title: hordeum vulgare var. spontaneum
intro: Hordeum vulgare var. spontaneum, also known as wild einkorn or two-horned barley, is a subspecies of the Hordeum vulgare plant that is native to the Middle East and South Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Hordeum vulgare var. spontaneum include its ability to help lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tea or tincture, and its seeds can be ground into flour for use in baking.
